LAW AND MOTION 302, NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: PLAINTIFF HORNEFF WADDLE CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. PLAINTIFF CONCEDES THIS IS A MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. HOWEVER, SUCH A MOTION WAS NOT NOTICED AND THE COURT MAY NOT CONSIDER PLAINTIFF'S MOTION. GONZALES V. SUPERIOR COURT (1987) 189 CAL. APP. 3D 1542. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER. (302/PJM) JUDGE: PATRICK MAHONEY; REPORTER: KIMBERLY JENNINGS, CSR 11452
MINUTES FOR AUG-11-2008 9:00 AM FOR DEPT 602

THIS CASE COMES ON CALENDAR, HAVING BEEN ASSIGNED BY THE PRESIDING JUDGE, FOR BENCH TRIAL. PLAINTIFF'S CASE; PLAINTIFF RESTS. DEFENDANT'S CASE; DEFENSE RESTS. CLOSING/REBUTTAL ARGUMENTS MADE. COURT ORDERS JUDGMENT FOR PLAINTIFF AND AGAINST DEFENDANT ASANO IN THE AMOUNT OF $7,700.00 PLUS ATTORNEYS FEES AND COSTS PER MEMORANDUM OF COSTS. COUNSEL FOR PLAINTIFF IS ORDERED TO SUBMIT PROPOSED JUDGMENT. JUDGE SUZANNE BOLANOS, CSR: PEPPINA THOMPSON, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA (602).

Off Calendar Aug-11-2008 Assigned To Trial Court

COURT TRIAL
Off Calendar Aug-11-2008 Text Ruling

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Jul-11-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Plaintiff Horneff Waddle Corporation's Motion for Summary Judgment is denied. Plaintiff concedes this is a motion for summary adjudication. However, such a motion was not noticed and the Court may not consider plaintiff's motion. Gonzales v. Superior Court (1987) 189 Cal. App. 3d 1542. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order. (302/PJM/PB) Judge: Patrick Mahoney; Reporter: Kimberly Jennings, CSR 11452

MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT JOEL REIDY'S MOTION To Quash Summons Of Complaint.
APPEARANCES: Donald W. Detisch, Esq. of Law Offices of Donald Detisch 619/515-1140 for deft & moving party (appearing via CourtCall); no other appearances.
RULING: Defendant's counsel did not comply with the tentative ruling rule (re giving proper notice to opposing party of his intent to appear at the hearing), therefore the Court adopted its tentative ruling - GRANT REQUEST TO CONTINUE TO FEB. 14, 2008 FOR THE PURPOSE OF CONDUCTING DISCOVERY ON JUDISDICTIONAL ISSUES.
